tossups.question: In 1973, a conductor from this country gave a six-part lecture series comparing classical music to concepts in linguistics. Under the guidance of Arthur Fiedler, a composer from this country wrote light music such as “The Syncopated Clock.” A production from this country pairs a scene of dancing animals with a musical outtake from Act III of La Gioconda, the “Dance of the Hours.” This country’s first direct-to-television opera was (*) Gian Carlo Menotti’s Amahl and the Night Visitors and its leading radio orchestra was directed for fourteen years by Arturo Toscanini. The Young People’s Concerts series originated in this country. This country produced a 1940 animated film accompanied by Leopold Stokowski recordings of pieces such as The Sorcerer’s Apprentice. For 10 points, name this country where the movie Fantasia was produced by Walt Disney.
tossups.answer: United States of America [or U.S.A; accept any underlined portion]
tossups.answer_sanitized: United States of America [or U.S.A; accept any underlined portion]
tossups.question_sanitized: In 1973, a conductor from this country gave a six-part lecture series comparing classical music to concepts in linguistics. Under the guidance of Arthur Fiedler, a composer from this country wrote light music such as "The Syncopated Clock." A production from this country pairs a scene of dancing animals with a musical outtake from Act III of La Gioconda, the "Dance of the Hours." This country's first direct-to-television opera was (*) Gian Carlo Menotti's Amahl and the Night Visitors and its leading radio orchestra was directed for fourteen years by Arturo Toscanini. The Young People's Concerts series originated in this country. This country produced a 1940 animated film accompanied by Leopold Stokowski recordings of pieces such as The Sorcerer's Apprentice. For 10 points, name this country where the movie Fantasia was produced by Walt Disney.

bonuses.leadin: In the opera A Quiet Place, Dede protects one of these places previously owned by his mother Dinah before Old Sam appears. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In the opera A Quiet Place, Dede protects one of these places previously owned by his mother Dinah before Old Sam appears. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these places, one of which titles the finale of an operetta whose title character sings “You’ve been a fool, and so have I” before resolving to marry a woman who earlier sings “Glitter and Be Gay.”
The opera A Quiet Place is by this American composer who included “Make Our Garden Grow” in his operetta Candide and who wrote the music for West Side Story.

Trouble in Tahiti, in which Dinah sings the aria “There is a Garden,” achieved success after being performed by an opera theater named for this company, which also commissioned Amahl and the Night Visitors.
parts_sanitized: Name these places, one of which titles the finale of an operetta whose title character sings "You've been a fool, and so have I" before resolving to marry a woman who earlier sings "Glitter and Be Gay."
The opera A Quiet Place is by this American composer who included "Make Our Garden Grow" in his operetta Candide and who wrote the music for West Side Story.

Trouble in Tahiti, in which Dinah sings the aria "There is a Garden," achieved success after being performed by an opera theater named for this company, which also commissioned Amahl and the Night Visitors.
answers_sanitized: gardens [accept "Make Our Garden Grow"; accept "There is a Garden"]
NBC [or National Broadcasting Company; or NBC Opera Theatre]

bonuses.leadin: The great music critic Dave Hurwitz once claimed that a piece of music by this composer would have made for “the greatest five minutes of ice skating.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The great music critic Dave Hurwitz once claimed that a piece of music by this composer would have made for "the greatest five minutes of ice skating." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer who composed the ballet excerpt Medea’s Dance of Vengeance. This composer’s first solo piano piece was Excursions.
Barber was romantic partners with this other composer known for operas like The Consul and the first opera composed for television, Amahl and the Night Visitors.
Samuel Barber’s most performed work, Adagio for Strings, was broadcast over the radio after the assassination of this American President in 1963.
parts_sanitized: Name this composer who composed the ballet excerpt Medea's Dance of Vengeance. This composer's first solo piano piece was Excursions.

answers_sanitized: Samuel Barber [or Samuel Osmond Barber II]
John F. Kennedy [or JFK; or John Fitzgerald Kennedy]

bonuses.leadin: Members of this orchestra comprise most of the Mostly Mozart Festival Orchestra, which often performs at the Lincoln Center. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this orchestra. The oldest of the “Big Five” orchestras, this orchestra is currently led by Jaap van Zweden [“YOP fon ZWAY-din”] and has historically been the home of such conductors as Gustav Mahler and Leonard Bernstein [“BURN-styne”].

This orchestra, one of the most prominent in the “Golden Age” of American classical music, was formed specifically for the conductor Arturo Toscanini. Although not part of the “Big Five,” this radio orchestra was originally disbanded in 1954 but was reformed and premiered Amahl and the Night Visitors.

The successor the NBC Symphony Orchestra, the Symphony of the Air, was led by this conductor. This Polish émigré is most famous for leading the Philadelphia Orchestra and notably orchestrated many works, including the Toccata and Fugue and Pictures at an Exhibition.
answers_sanitized: NBC Symphony Orchestra [prompt on partial answer]

This orchestra, one of the most prominent in the "Golden Age" of American classical music, was formed specifically for the conductor Arturo Toscanini. Although not part of the "Big Five," this radio orchestra was originally disbanded in 1954 but was reformed and premiered Amahl and the Night Visitors.

The successor the NBC Symphony Orchestra, the Symphony of the Air, was led by this conductor. This Polish emigre is most famous for leading the Philadelphia Orchestra and notably orchestrated many works, including the Toccata and Fugue and Pictures at an Exhibition.

bonuses.leadin: In the final act of an opera, Magda leaves this location to go home and kill herself. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this unnamed location where many people are waiting. A magician performs magic tricks to impress the secretary here, and Magda’s husband, the dissident John Sorel, is eventually captured after showing up here.
The consul’s office is a major setting in The Consul, an opera by this man. He composed the first opera specifically made for television.
Menotti’s television opera, Amahl and the Night Visitors, is about a crippled boy named Amahl who is visited by the Magi, three kings bearing gifts intended for this recent newborn.

bonuses.leadin: This composer used a subject beginning E-flat G-flat B-flat D in a four-voice fugue that constitutes the last movement of his piano sonata. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this American composer of Knoxville: Summer of 1915, who adapted a movement from his B minor string quartet into Adagio for Strings.

Barber wrote three of these orchestral works, the first of which was premiered by Toscanini alongside Adagio for Strings. These works, named after a literary form, develop out of a single melodic theme.
Barber lived in a house called Capricorn with this composer of Amahl and the Night Visitors and The Saint of Bleecker Street. This man also founded the Spoleto Festivals.
answers_sanitized: Samuel Barber [or Samuel Osborne Barber II]

parts: The title child of this opera asks Balthazar, Kaspar, and Melchior about the gifts they are bringing.
Amahl and the Night Visitors was written by this composer of The Old Man and the Thief, a radio opera, as well as The Consul.

answers: woman [do NOT accept “castrato”, prompt on “soprano” or “mezzo-soprano”, prompt on “adult]

bonuses.leadin: The title figure’s mother tries to steal gold intended for the Christ child for her son. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera about the title figure’s encounter with the Three Kings. It was the first opera composed for television broadcast.
This Italian composer wrote Amahl and the Night Visitors. He also won the Pulitzer Prize for the operas The Consul and The Saint of Bleecker Street.

When King Melchior tells Amahl and his mother that the king they will meet won’t require gifts of gold, they seek to honor the Christ child by sending gifts. Amahl decides to give him this item in thanks for being healed.
answers: his crutch [accept reasonable equivalents such as a staff]

bonuses.leadin: This aria is sung by the mother of the title character. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this aria, in which that woman wonders how a group of gift givers cannot understand the troubles of the poor and proceeds to be caught while stealing some of the namesake substance.
“All that Gold!” and “This is my box” are arias from this opera, in which the title character is cured of an ailment after giving a gift of his only possession to King Melchior.
Amahl and the Night Visitors was written by this composer, who also wrote an opera about a woman who has religious visions, The Saint of Bleecker Street.

bonuses.leadin: It is followed by the song “Thief! Thief!,” which is sung by the page of the kings of the Orient. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this song sung by Amahl’s mother in Amahl and the Night Visitors after she sees the gifts brought by the Magi for Jesus.
Rosemary Kuhlmann sang “All That Gold” in Amahl and the Night Visitors, an opera by this life partner of Samuel Barber.
Kuhlmann also sang the role of Desideria in this Menotti opera which tells the story of Annina, a girl living in New York’s Little Italy who has stigmata.

bonuses.leadin: The central dialogue of this opera is often interrupted due to the poor quality of the French telephone system. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this one-act opera in which the only character, the woman Elle, talks to her lover on the telephone. With a libretto by Jean Cocteau, it was its composer’s last opera.

This member of Les Six composed The Human Voice. He also adapted a surrealist play by Guillaume Apollinaire for his opera The Breasts of Tiresias, and a Georges Bernanos screenplay for his opera Dialogues of the Carmelites.
Another one-act opera that involves a telephone is the appropriately-named The Telephone, which was composed by this American who included the three Magi in his opera Amahl and the Night Visitors.
answers_sanitized: The Human Voice [or La voix humaine]
